title: hieracium atratum subsp. orthopodum
intro: Hieracium atratum subsp. orthopodum, also known as the lesser hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Hieracium atratum subsp. orthopodum have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used topically to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
